Adam and Josh are expected to protect their boss' gold shipment, and to provide safe conduct for Osman Bey's three daughters. Along the way, our 'heroes' decide to forget their mission and abscond with the gold, but their plans are foiled by their own inherent ineptitude--and by the bothersome interference of duplicitous Colonel Elci.

Adam and Josh are expected to protect their boss' gold shipment, and to provide safe conduct for Osman Bey's three daughters. Along the way, our 'heroes' decide to forget their mission and abscond with the gold, but their plans are foiled by their own inherent ineptitude--and by the bothersome interference of duplicitous Colonel Elci.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The day begins with a powerful dose of perspective as contestants hear real-life breaching stories from American heroes—reminders of the stakes they’re preparing for. The team is then introduced to a concealed firearm headrest safe designed for rapid-response vehicle defense. Next, it’s straight into the action with a preliminary course of fire using GreenLight sights, where top performers are pushed even further with a bonus round to test speed, accuracy, and composure under pressure. The intensity ramps up during a sentry scenario featuring SABRE CO₂ non-lethal cartridges, putting their stealth and judgment to the test. But just as the dust begins to settle, a new CIA briefing drops—and it’s the most dire yet. With mission reality closing in fast, the contestants close the day with a crash course in S.E.R.E. (Survival, Evasion, Resistance, and Escape) training, rounding out their classroom combat readiness with hard-earned grit.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: Kyle and Caleb give the skinny on the hamstring pull that was heard around the world. From there, Surviving Mann fans get a glimpse at just how far Faraday containers can go to protect your security, privacy and device. After, Black Aces does a deep dive into their available products and some exciting plans for the future.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The wrath of Mann is upon the contestants as Don doles out punishments to the entire squad for one person’s mistake. On the tails of the punishment, Don leads everyone in a lesson on BUD/S training. Later, co-host and retired Green Beret, Mike Glover, gives the contestants a life lesson on survival they won’t soon forget. To top off the episode, one of the contestants demonstrates his industry-changing hollow point bullets in some meaty targets.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: In this episode, competitor James gives us an update on how his injuries are progressing and how he’s able to push through when most thought he would be long gone. Luckily for the competitors, he had a special demonstration ready that involved hollow points and gel blocks. Later, Matthew recaps his experience with “The Glove”. To cap things off, Chris also sits down with BodyHz to talk about innovations in the non-chemical health industry.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The Glove Returns with a vengeance! This crop of contestants is the best of the best.. but how do they stack up against The Glove? Where about to find out. After The Glove waves goodbye, it’s hot-headed friend makes a debut courtesy of Exothermic Technologies. The contestants have a hard time keeping a smile off their faces as they shoot at targets and then blow them up.

programme.title: Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show
programme.desc: On this special Father's Day Episode of the Mitchell Pehlke Lacrosse Show presented by Duke Cannon, we interviewed four famous lacrosse dads. Joe Spallina describes what it's like to raise potentially five D1 lacrosse players, Paul Carcaterra shares the trick that helped his daughter Peyla became one of the top girls lacrosse players in the country, Greg Kenneally (ECD Greg) details one invention idea to make parenting easier, and Matt Hasselbeck explains why so many NFL quarterbacks become Lacrosse Dads.

programme.title: Through X

With a National title, the Tewaaraton Award winner, and record shattering season – she has cemented herself as an all-time great. We also went to Charlottesville, VA to dissect the elite game of Connor Shellenberger: a hometown hero, and arguably the best player in history for the University of Virginia.

programme.title: Forbidden Trails 1941
programme.desc: The Rough Riders ride again in Forbidden Trails! When Marshal Buck Roberts (Buck Jones) is gunned down and badly wounded, he refuses to quit the fight. With fellow lawmen Tim McCoy and Raymond Hatton at his side, the trio saddles up to take on a ruthless gang terrorizing the frontier. Packed with blazing six-gun action, camaraderie, and justice served Western-style, this thrilling chapter in the Rough Riders series delivers nonstop excitement.

programme.title: Law of Vengeance
programme.desc: 'Law and Order' may not have been in its absolute prime yet, understandable though as it was still early days at this point. The pre-Briscoe seasons and episodes were still good though, with some fine episodes and not really any that one would call a misfire

programme.title: Massacre Time 1966
programme.desc: In Massacre Time, Franco Nero blazes onto the Spaghetti Western scene as Tom Corbett, a drifter who returns home to find his family’s land stolen and his brother living in ruin. Together, the two face a ruthless tyrant and his sadistic son who rule with terror and bloodshed. Directed by Lucio Fulci, this violent, gripping tale delivers brutal gunfights, raw emotion, and the relentless pursuit of vengeance.

programme.title: Fugitive Valley (1941) Colorized
programme.desc: In Fugitive Valley, the Rough Riders ride again! When an outlaw gang takes refuge in a hidden valley and begins terrorizing nearby ranchers, U.S. Marshals Buck Roberts (Buck Jones), Tim McCall (Tim McCoy), and Sandy Hopkins (Raymond Hatton) saddle up to bring them down. With six-guns blazing, daring stunts, and the trio’s trademark camaraderie, this action-packed Western delivers frontier justice straight to the heart of outlaw country.

programme.title: Primarily Primates
programme.desc: In this episode we meet human's closest relatives - primates. See what makes a chimpanzee one of the most intelligent primates. Find out why the spider monkey is such an amazing climber and feel the power of the howler monkey's howl.

programme.title: Surf and Turf
programme.desc: In this episode we'll find out how sharks use electro-sensors to detect their prey. Also, discover the skunk's stinky defense mechanism. And domestic versus wild - see how the striped zebra matches up against its cousin the horse.

programme.title: Water, Water Everywhere
programme.desc: Join host Nick Uhas and his guests as they dive deep into the wonderful world of water. In this week's experiments we discover how water can start a fire, transform breakfast cereal into a magnetic field detector, and even change the way you visualize the world around you.

programme.title: Barcode
programme.desc: Barcodes have radically changed global commerce, helping fuel the rise of everything from massive companies like Walmart to world powers like China. And it all started with a man daydreaming on a beach in 1949.

programme.title: Photowalks On Scripps News
programme.desc: Lifelong photographer and journalist Jefferson Graham shows off the most photographable places through the lens of his smartphone.
